data.label: Inventory dependencies
data.prompt:
You are taking inventory of a project's dependencies before a security audit.

<task>
Read any dependency manifests in the working directory: `package.json`, `Cargo.toml`, `pyproject.toml`, `go.mod`, `Gemfile`, etc. Do not run any installer or network command — just read the files.
</task>

For each manifest you find, list its direct dependencies in this format:

<manifest>
  <path>relative path to the file</path>
  <ecosystem>npm | cargo | pip | go | gem | other</ecosystem>
  <deps>
    - name@version — runtime | dev
  </deps>
</manifest>

After the manifests, add a short **Suspicious** section listing any dep that:
- Looks abandoned (very old version pin)
- Is a known-risky package (eval-based, unmaintained crypto, etc.)
- Has a name you don't recognize

If nothing is suspicious, write "Suspicious: none" on its own line.

data.label: CVE pass
data.prompt:
You are a security engineer auditing the dependency inventory below for known CVEs and supply-chain risk.

<inventory>
{{previous.output}}
</inventory>

For each dependency that has a known vulnerability or notable risk, emit:

<finding>
  <severity>critical | high | medium | low</severity>
  <package>name@version</package>
  <ecosystem>npm | cargo | pip | go | gem | other</ecosystem>
  <issue>CVE id or one-line description of the risk</issue>
  <fix>upgrade to X.Y.Z, replace with alternative, or remove</fix>
</finding>

Do not invent CVE ids. If you are unsure whether a version is affected, mark severity `low` and say so in the issue. If nothing is at risk, say "No vulnerable dependencies found." on a single line.

data.label: Prioritized actions
data.prompt:
Distill the dependency audit findings below into a prioritized action list a maintainer can work through top-to-bottom.

<content>
{{previous.output}}
</content>

Format the response as:

- **TL;DR**: one sentence on overall dependency health
- **Now**: bullets for critical/high findings, each starting with a verb (`Upgrade`, `Replace`, `Remove`)
- **Soon**: bullets for medium findings
- **Watch**: bullets for low findings worth monitoring

Omit any section that has no items. Each bullet must name the package and the concrete next step.

data.label: Human sign-off
data.prompt: Read the prioritized action list above. Approve to end the workflow, or reject if the audit looks incomplete and should be re-run.
